description: Examine, analyze, and interpret accounting records to prepare financial statements, give advice, or audit and evaluate statements prepared by others. Install or advise on systems of recording costs or other financial and budgetary data. Examine budget estimates for completeness, accuracy, and conformance with procedures and regulations. Analyze budgeting and accounting reports. Prepare tax returns for individuals or small businesses. (Based on 15 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Conduct safety drills, such as man overboard or fire drills.
Signal crew members to rig tow lines, open or close gates, or pull guard chains across entries.
Read gauges to verify sufficient levels of hydraulic fluid, air pressure, or oxygen.
Inspect vessels to ensure safe operation and compliance with regulations.
Stand watches on vessels during specified periods while vessels are under way.
Dock or undock vessels, maneuvering through narrow spaces, such as locks.
Operate ship-to-shore radios to exchange information needed for ship operations.
Steer and operate vessels, using radios, depth finders, radars, lights, or buoys.
Consult maps, charts, weather reports, or navigation equipment for ship movements.
Direct courses and speeds of ships based on knowledge of local winds, weather, tides, and hazards.
Provide assistance in maritime rescue operations.
Signal passing vessels using whistles, flashing lights, flags, or radios.
Measure depths of water using depth-measuring equipment.
Maintain boats or onboard equipment such as engines, navigational systems, or life preservers.
Maintain records of daily activities, ship positions, and weather conditions.
Observe cargo loading or unloading to ensure specifications are met.
Perform or participate in emergency drills as required.
Monitor operations of engines or equipment to identify malfunctions.
Install engine controls, propeller shafts, or propellers.
Monitor availability and condition of lifesaving equipment to ensure compliance with regulations.
Act as a liaison between the ship's captain and shore personnel to maintain schedules and budgets.
Supervise engineers and crew members and train them for routine and emergency duties.
Investigate and observe tests on machinery and equipment for compliance with standards.
Inspect marine equipment and machinery to draw up work requests and job specifications.
Conduct environmental, operational, or performance tests on marine machinery and equipment.
Evaluate operation of marine equipment during acceptance testing and shakedown cruises.
Evaluate performance of craft during dock and sea trials to determine design changes and conformance with standards.
Study design proposals and specifications to establish basic characteristics of craft, such as size, weight, and propulsion.
Prepare plans, estimates, and specifications, including special provisions, for marine constructions or modifications.
Perform monitoring activities to ensure that ships comply with international regulations and standards for safety and pollution prevention.
Confer with clients, contractors, or other personnel to exchange information or resolve problems related to marine operations.
Inspect or test materials, vehicles, or equipment to ensure they are free of defects and consistent with specifications.
Inspect work areas or operating equipment to ensure conformance to established standards such as cleanliness or maintenance.
Inspect materials, stock, vehicles, equipment, or facilities to ensure they are safe, free of defects, and consistent with specifications.
Train workers in proper operational procedures, explain company policies, and assign training tasks to new or experienced workers.
Enforce safety rules and regulations to ensure compliance and worker safety.
Direct or coordinate the activities of workers, such as flight or car attendants, to maintain efficient operations.
Confer with customers, supervisors, contractors, or personnel to exchange information or resolve problems.
Compute or estimate cash, payroll, transportation, or personnel requirements for operational planning.
